Most of the books I read give examples using printf and scanf. At some point the students know perfectly how to use these two functions but they don't know about stdin, stdout and argv. To me and according to many programming principles (e.g. KISS) a program should not interrupt the execution for prompting the user. Instead, and this is a much clever approach, the developer should learn to use the power of pipes and use the options and the arguments. I think this: $ whatdaywas 1982 02 16 Monday Is much better than: $ whatdaywas Enter a year: 1982 Enter a month: 2 Enter a day: 16 It was a Monday. Is there any rationale behind this pedagogical approach?
